title: calystegia malacophylla var. berryi
intro: Calystegia malacophylla var. berryi, also known as the Hawaiian bindweed or Berry's false morning glory, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to Hawaii. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calystegia malacophylla var. berryi include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, although it is essential to note that the plant's availability and use are heavily regulated due to its endangered status.
